response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering credentials and 20+ years of professional experience, he is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. His C++ experience is dormant—last used in 2015 at National Instruments for 6 months—whereas the role demands active hands-on C++ development as a primary skill. More critically, he has zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, or financial services domains. His entire career has focused on web development, GIS platforms, and distributed systems. Reviving stale C++ skills would require significant ramp-up, and the absence of financial domain knowledge presents an additional barrier.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software engineering experience with demonstrated capability across multiple domains and languages
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing skills evidenced by workshops, public talks, and open-source contributions
Solid foundational understanding of software architecture, testing practices (TDD), and CI/CD infrastructure
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated—last hands-on C++ work in 2015; current skill set centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No exposure to Windows platform development; entire recent career is cloud-based and web-focused (AWS, React, MongoDB)
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain experience

key_factors.factor: Current C++ proficiency and Windows platform experience
